extra.reasoning: The property is globally(opted_out implies not sent), i.e., the bad state opted_out=true AND sent=true must be unreachable. Analysis of transitions: (1) sent is set to true ONLY by t2 (send_attempt with guard not opted_out), which requires opted_out=false. (2) opted_out is set to true only by t1, which simultaneously sets sent:=false. (3) opted_out is monotonic: no transition (t1/t2/t3/t4) ever sets opted_out back to false. Therefore, once opted_out=true, it remains true forever, and the only transition that could set sent:=true (t2) is permanently disabled (its guard not opted_out is false). While opted_out=true, send_attempt triggers t3 (sent:=false), tick triggers t4 (sent:=false), and repeated optout t1 keeps sent:=false. No enabled transition can produce sent=true in any opted_out=true state. The send_attempt guards (t2: not opted_out; t3: opted_out) are mutually exclusive, so there is no priority/conflict ambiguity. Hence the bad state is unreachable on every execution (independent of length, well within the 7-event bound), and the property holds.
